records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Encompassed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Encompassing.]

defs: To circumscribe or go round so as to surround closely; to encircle; to inclose; to environ; as, a ring encompasses the finger; an army encompasses a city; a voyage encompassing the world.
quotes: A question may be encompassed with difficulty.
The love of all thy sons encompass thee.
